3.why: No character has been verified across all strings yet.
3.invariant: prefix contains only characters known to be common.

4.happening: Store the first string as the reference string.
4.why: Every common prefix character must match the same position in this first string.

5.happening: Set the initial maximum prefix length to the first string length.
5.why: A prefix cannot be longer than the first string.
5.invariant: min_len is an upper bound on the answer length.

6.happening: Move through the list to inspect string index {k}.
6.why: The shortest string controls the maximum possible prefix length.
6.invariant: All strings already checked are at least min_len characters long.

7.happening: Load the current string from the array.
7.why: Its length may lower the prefix limit.
7.invariant: s is the string currently being measured.

8.why: The algorithm needs this length to keep the scan inside every string.

9.happening: The current string is shorter than the saved limit.
9.why: The prefix cannot extend beyond this shorter string.
9.invariant: min_len must shrink to remain safe.

9.happening: The current string does not reduce the limit.
9.why: The existing min_len is still the shortest length seen.
9.invariant: min_len remains a safe upper bound.

10.happening: Update min_len to the current shorter length.
10.why: Only positions before this length can be checked safely.
10.invariant: min_len equals the shortest string length seen so far.

11.why: A common prefix is verified one column at a time.
11.invariant: All earlier positions are already confirmed.

12.happening: Read the reference character from the first string.
12.why: Every other string must match this character at position {i}.
12.invariant: ch is the expected character for this column.

13.happening: Move to string index {j} for comparison.
13.why: The current column must match in every string.
13.invariant: Strings before index {j} have matched this column.

14.why: The next step compares this string's character with the reference.
14.invariant: current is the string currently being checked.

15.happening: Read the character at position {i} from the current string.
15.why: This is the character that must match the first string's character.
15.invariant: other is the current string's candidate character.

16.happening: The current character does not match the reference character.
16.why: A mismatch means this column and all later columns cannot be common.
16.invariant: The answer is the prefix built before this mismatch.

16.happening: The current character matches the reference character.
16.why: This string still agrees with the current column.
16.invariant: The column remains valid for all strings checked so far.

17.happening: Store the prefix built before the mismatch.
17.why: That prefix is the longest part verified across every earlier column.
17.invariant: result now holds the final answer.

18.why: The first mismatch proves the common prefix cannot be extended.
18.invariant: No later character can change the answer.

19.happening: Append the matched character to the prefix.
19.why: Every string matched this character position.
19.invariant: prefix is still common to every string.

20.happening: Store the completed prefix as the result.
20.why: All safe character positions were checked without an invalid column.
20.invariant: result contains the longest common prefix.

21.why: The scan has checked every possible shared position.
21.invariant: The returned string is common to all input strings.

init.why: The algorithm needs a prefix, a baseline string, and a safe length limit.

loop.why: The algorithm checks strings and character positions systematically.

check.why: A shorter string or mismatch determines where the prefix can stop.
check.invariant: Only safe characters can enter the prefix.

update.why: The algorithm records a new limit, prefix, or result after a check.
update.invariant: Stored state remains consistent with verified input.

return.why: The longest common prefix is known.
